While we strive to maintain the highest customer satisfaction standards, monitoring our effectiveness can be a challenge at times. reviews.title: Pretty disappointed
reviews.text:
The room we were provided was not how it looked in the photos. There is no pool, just a gym and the place is pretty run down, they allow  people to smoke in the hallways so the place smells of cigarettes. 

When we booked the room, they charged us for the full payment, when we checked in, they told us that they would refund that payment and required us to pay a deposit of 3.5 mil for our room, and then on top of it, they charged us the full payment of our room again. So we ended up getting charged 3 times! What a ridiculous hotel policy. 

The staff are bored and unfriendly. The area is far from town and far from everything. I would not recommend nor be coming back to stay here again.
reviews.response:
Dear Mylan,
We are sorry to hear of your disappointment during your recent stay with us. Thank you for giving us the opportunity to address your concerns.
At Capri, we do have both smoking and non-smoking floors to ensure that our guests have a most enjoyable and comfortable stay with us. Should a non-smoking room be required, our guests are encouraged to put up a request upon making the reservation.
We would like to clarify that it is our practice to take a pre-authorized amount prior to arrival date and we wish to highlight that this was not a sale transaction on your credit card. Instead, this amount was held against your credit limit, and it was released upon the check-in process. We are sorry if it was not clearly made known to you and we believe that the payment matter was properly resolved.
Mylan, we are sorry if we had failed to meet your expectations during your recent stay with us. We hope to be able to welcome you again and have another opportunity to serve you better.

reviews.title: Overpriced and overstated
reviews.text: Stayed for 3 nights because of attending tradeshow at nearby SECC. Thoroughly disappointed. Agoda sold the room as One Bedroom Deluxe Apartment and charged a whopping S$539 . It's anything but deluxe even at this age of over-exaggeration. It is clean and tidy for sure but very basic. The breakfast was particularly subpar, bare minimum choice and quality of food hardly even keeping up with a 3 star establishment standard. Staff at the breakfast appeared to be totally disengaged and disinterested. Can't believe it has a Fraser name to it. The only saving grace was the friendly front desk staff. Not recommended for short term stayers unless absolutely need to be close to SECC.
reviews.response:
Dear Mr. Yan,

Thank you very much for your candid review of your recent stay at our hotel. Your feedback is extremely valuable to us, as we strive to improve with each and every stay. 

On behalf of the team, Allow me to apologize for the short fall service you receive in your last stay. I understand that the quality of the room and service are not meet up with your expectation from a Fraser property and indeed need intensive view on it.

Regarding this case, I already took some action with the F&B Team to make sure the proper quality standard. 

It would be such a pleasure to personally welcome you back to our hotel and show you the service that you that can satisfy your expectation!

With much appreciation,

Lance Luong
Front Office Supervisor

reviews.title: Not as expected
reviews.text: I felt this place was overrated. The place we rented was not the same as shown in the photo. Due to a family emergency, we required an additional bed. The dish washing sponge provided in the kitchen was so old that it had mold on it. I felt disgusted that they washed our remaining spoons and cups that I went to Cresent Mall's Giant supermarket to buy my own dish washing detergent as well as a schwangling new dish washing sponge. Room service did not clean the air conditioner filters and I had to clean it myself to get the room to a bearable temperature. My sister slipped in the laundry "spin" room late at night after they had a leaking washing machine. She hurt herself and we contacted reception to inform them of the incident. We weren't compensated.

reviews.title: Nice Infrastructure wasted by an horrible service
reviews.text:
Breakfast quality was not good, very bad smell around the Vietnamese soup station / coffee and tea self service / Unfriendly and  untrained service staff. 
I have been woke up at 9.00AM by an Housekeeping or Front Office staff, ringing by 10 times the bell door, once i opened the door he asked me if I was checking out on that day, i answered no; Then he said oh, sorry sir and left. What kind of service is that?
Housekeeping came at 2.00PM to clean the room after requesting it one hour before.
Food Room service choice was very limited, and the food average.
One evening, i asked for a fruit juice and some bread at room service, the answer i got was "Impossible sir, we have a party tonight!" Come on guys....
Check Out took over 30 minutes because of waiting for Housekeeping to check the room.
Overall, it was disappointing as Fraser name we were expecting a 4 star International standard at least and got a Vietnamese 2 star service in a 4 star Infrastructure hotel. That's a pity. A big improvement is needed in this place or focus on groups and refuse FIT's.

reviews.title: Poor hotel management
reviews.text: I stayed in the room for one night (last Sat) to join the marathon. The location is great, very close to the venue. Upon entering the room, I felt that the room was dark and hot. I found out that some lights were broken and the air con wasn't working properly. The hotel sent an engineer to repair them for us. Later before the midnight, the air con was broken. I phoned the receptionist for help and he sent the engineer to come up again. This time the engineer stayed in the room for 30 mins or so in order to repair the air con. He made a lot of noise and we stayed awake till midnight. The following morning, we needed to wake up at 3am. To my surprise, the hotel didn't follow it up or apologize for the poor maintenance of the rooms. I don't think the room nor the service is worth the money (we paid almost USD 90 for this small, dark and hot room).

reviews.title: Relaxing Place / Helpful Staff / Far from Centre
reviews.text:
The room was a bit different from the photos on the Internet.  It was still big and clean, but it did not look very warm.  However, it became great when my wife fell sick on the 1st night.  The location was close to the hospital and there was an open kitchen which was well-equipped with basic cooking utensils and facilities.  That could enable me to warm congee for my wife.  The room itself was spacious, like a simple modern home.

The breakfast lounge was also nice.  It is a good place to talk and stay.  

In general, the hotel itself is decent.  Staff were always there to help when there are problems in the room.  But you have to note that the location is distant from the city centre and it takes around 40 minutes to reach the centre, but taxis are always there opposite to the hotel.  It's easy and convenient.  There is nothing much around the hotel aside from one shopping central and the family mart.

reviews.text:
Overall, it was a pretty decent hotel. The plus points was that the hotel is near the Convention Center in District 7 (5 - 8 mins walking distance) and the breakfast was quite good. 

Unfortunately, there are no street food (only restaurants) around that area thus F&B are pretty pricey around the area. 

Journey to District 1 would take around 30 - 45 mins (by taxi / Grab) depending on traffic. 

The hotel was alright for a business hotel but the location in District 7 meant that it will only be for attendees to the convention center.

reviews.text:
Warm welcome from front desk service staff made me feel good upon arrival, especially travel more than an hour from airport. Yes, the location and traffic are undesirable.
The room, however, was clean and neat. It was pleasant.
The only setback was food and dining. Not much of a choice for dinner, and had to take sushi next door. Breakfast variety was also limited. Service staff at breakfast were not attentive, as I had to wait endlessly right from the doorstep and find my own coffee.
In conclusion, please improve on food and dining at Capri HCM.

reviews.text:

The staff at the restaurant just said sorry, not even in a sincere way, and apparently did realized what I just suffered because of their incompetence and negligence. I would have filed a formal complaint if I had had the time.
reviews.response:

We are sorry for the inconvenient you had experienced due to our shortfall in cleaning the area and the staff attitude. This incident is something we deeply regret and our guest safety is one of our most concerning matter at all time. Sincerely, 
Lance Luong, Front Office Supervisor.

reviews.title: New hotel, work in progress
reviews.text:
One good thing about this hotel is its away from all the horns of the motorbikes in the more popular District 1 area...The hotel offers free 1 way shuttle into the area at 4 timings a day and after awhile in the area, the constant horning just gets to you... no trip back though and you'd need to take a cab back.....taxis were constantly available outside the hotel so jsut ask the reception and they would flag one almost immediately for you...

One thing was the reception also double hatted as the concierge and i would imagine as teh hotel got more busy that they would be swamped.......they were all very friendly and eager to help and most requests were settled well...just need some time for them to get more experienced and polish and it'd be perfect...nothing to complain about though

overall the hotel is almost done..there was some renovations going on when we were trying to check in but no problems with that....the rooms were nice..just one minor suggestion would be to replace the white lights in the lobby and walkways which were quite harsh (and made the place look quite shabby) to a more classy and warm light.

reviews.title: Comfortable stay
reviews.text: Stayed at Capri By Fraser HCM for a business trip along with my colleague. One of the reasons why we chose this hotel was because we were attending an exhibition at the Saigon Exhibition and Convention Centre. It was a quick 5-10 min walk from the hotel. There is a high end mall (CGV Parkson Paragon) near the hotel, you can head over to the basement foodcourt for food. If you have more time in the evening, you can walk all the way to The Crescent, which is a huge mall that is along the river. There are lots of cafes and restaurants along the river where you can spend some time. There is also a huge supermarket there. The hotel is in district 7 which has a high population of South Koreans, so you will find alot of korean items at the supermarket and also shops.

reviews.title: Average Novotel Stay
reviews.text:
Stayed at Novotel recently. Overall, it’s alright, clean enough and serviceable, but nothing special. 

The bigger issue is the room maintenance, facilities feel dated and poorly kept, and the fridge in my room didn’t work at all.  Not sure why the room was available for check-in if the fridge was faulty.

Acceptable for a short stay, but not what you’d expect from the brand unless maintenance improves.

reviews.title: Close to SECC
reviews.text:
As a solo traveller I decided to stay in a higher star hotel for my safety and comfort. Price is higher than what I would expect from HCM but decided to go ahead as I understand it’s the Deepavali weekend and prices are higher than usual.

Despite it being a 4 star property, I felt that on the whole it was under my expectations for a hotel under Accor.

Room cleanliness was disappointing - upon entering the room there was a bad musky smell that caused me to gag. Thankfully the cleaning staff was nearby and I managed to get them to spray some air freshener to make it more bearable.

Overall the property’s best advantage is its close proximity to SECC. Front desk was also helpful and welcoming.

reviews.title: Business trips around the D7 area are fine.
reviews.text: This time, we stayed for two nights, and based on Agoda's recommendation, we chose to stay in separate categories because it was cheaper. The first night, the cheaper category was sold out, so we stayed in an apartment-type room, which was just spacious, but the bathtub was inconvenient to use. However, the standard room on the second night had a kitchenette as well, and even without a bathtub, the shower room was spacious, making it more functional than the apartment-type room where you have to wash your body inside the bathtub.

Quite a large number of Unilever employees from the subcontinent are using the place for their business trips to Vietnam. Unfortunately they tend to smoke in their rooms and toilets thus causing the fire alarms to be set on and leading also to neighbors being subjected to the passive smoking, which is very unpleasant. From time to time they also can be rather noisy once come together.

reviews.title: Comfortable & Relaxed
reviews.text:
-Location: Walking distance to shopping centers (Parkson Paragon & Crescent Mall) and other cafes and restaurants. Also near to a large supermarket in the Crescent Mall basement, as well as 2 Megastar cineplexes in the upper floors of Parkson Paragon and Crescent Mall. Complimentary shuttle to city center through advance booking with front desk and many cabs just outside hotel, with bus service along nearby main road.

-Apartment: Roomy, comfortable and clean. Substantial number of English channels on tv and well-equipped kitchenette, with 2 free bottles of water replenished daily. Bathroom comes with Malin+Goetz amenities, which is nice. Only complaint is the lack of hanging bars/hooks in the bathroom--would be great if Management can at least add some hang bars or hooks around the wash basin area for face/hand towels. *hint* Other than this, stay was pleasant and relaxed.

-Breakfast@Caprilicious: Decent selection provided. Buffett includes a small selection of vegetables for making salad; 2 cheeses and some nuts; bread and toaster with individual-packed butter, jams and honey on the side; some hot items like grilled tomatoes, bacon and baked beans; an egg station; cereals; juices and fresh milk; plus a few others.
reviews.positives: Convenience, Comfort & Value in One Package

reviews.text:
My stay here has been very comfortable and its location has made it possible to walk to SECC. 

Great staff at the hotel, check in and out was smooth and they made sure that I got my shuttle to Ben Thanh Market on my check out day.

However, there are a few downsides to this hotel. I was actually given an upgrade to the one bedroom deluxe. Whilst the room was great with a kitchen, there was only one tv in the living room which meant no tv for the bedroom. Can't watch tv on the bed.

Also the bathroom amenities like the shampoo and bath gel are absolutely impossible to open. They are sealed and need to be pulled off (impossible). I ended up poking every single one that I needed with my pen.

reviews.title: A short stay
reviews.text: The location is in district 7. Unlike other districts, the govt is very strict with cleanliness and street vendors since this is near to the exhibition centre-meaning there will be a lot of foreigners/expats in this distrist. You don't see the hustle and bustle of other districts so, it depends what you are looking for- a quiet place to stay or to really experience HCMC. For me personally, I have travelled for leisure/business to HCMC so many times, i don't need the hustle and bustle. I love District 7 the most, it is a suitable place to be for me, I can walk around with ease at night alone and still be safe. I had very bad experiences with district 1 and I never went back though the place i was staying was really great (ascott) but it is the area that deters me from going back. With 2 malls and hypermarkets just 2 and 5 minutes walk away, it felt like home. There are 3 restaurant just located at the hotel area, connecting to ibis hotel as well. There is 2 mini mart and 1 coffee shop 1min away. The staffs are very friendly and speaks english well. If you need any help, they would be the one to go to. The room is as shown in the photos. The cooking area is great as the ventilator is very good, they don't leave any smell after you cooked.

There aren't many tourist attractions in Ho Chi Minh City, but since taxi fares are cheap, it's not a big issue to get around by taxi. Walking distance to the exhibition area. So this would be a reasonable price hotel in this area. Its an apartment style with two rooms, therefore, you can share one unit with friends or family.

Breakfast simple yet nice.

reviews.title: Nice place for business travellers
reviews.text: The CAPRI is a very nice place for business travelers. The hotel is very clean and so are the rooms. The gym is well equipped for a business traveler as well. The menu in the restaurant is a bit monotonous. It would be good to add some variety to the breakfast menu.
reviews.response:
Dear Prabha,

Thank you for your positive feedback, we glad to know that you like our rooms. We appreciated your recommend about the restaurant and is looking to it to improve immediately. Hope to see you again in your future trip to Ho Chi Minh.

Mija Park
General Manager

reviews.title: Good Location. Value for Money. Clean Rooms.
reviews.text: The hotel is located in District 7. There are 2 convenient stores nearby and 2 restaurants nearby. Parkson Shopping Mall is 3 min walking distance. The bigger malls such as SC Vivo City and Crescent mall are 5-10 min taxi ride. The rooms are clean and comfortable and it is a good value for money. This hotel is tucked away in a more quiet area, it is an ideal hotel for people who wants the convenience of near a shopping mall but some distance from the crowd.
reviews.positives: Good Location. Value for Money. Clean Rooms.
